privacy_disclaimer: Monero is a privacy-focused blockchain. By design, sender, receiver, and transaction amounts are obfuscated via ring signatures and stealth addresses. On-chain holder cohort analysis, whale tracking, exchange flow analysis, and smart money stance detection are IMPOSSIBLE and this is working as intended.
executive_summary: Monero's privacy properties are functioning as designed: holder cohort analysis is cryptographically impossible. Transaction count (25,000-41,000 daily) is the primary measurable metric alongside network hashrate; both demonstrate sustained network health and demand.

dau_metrics.note: DAU measured as daily transaction count (block explorers show transaction volumes but cannot identify senders/receivers or amounts)
dau_metrics.south_korea_activity_2025: 41% transaction rise driven by social media exposure and Asian trading

hashrate_metrics.note: Network hashrate is publicly visible and indicates mining activity/security

exchange_flows.delisting_impact: XMR delisted from major centralized exchanges over past few years for regulatory compliance; exchange volume tracking is now limited to remaining DEX and EU venue activity
exchange_flows.implication: Flow analysis unreliable; on-chain transaction activity is primary demand indicator

wash_trade_note: Not applicable. XMR is not traded on incentive-farming or high-manipulation venues.

retention_assessment.finding: Steady to rising daily transaction count (25k-41k range with upward pressure)
retention_assessment.interpretation: Healthy sustained demand despite CEX delistings; privacy-focused user base maintains consistent on-chain activity

unavailable_metrics.holder_cohort_analysis: IMPOSSIBLE - ring signatures + stealth addresses obfuscate all addresses
unavailable_metrics.address_clustering: IMPOSSIBLE - ring signatures hide participant identity
unavailable_metrics.exchange_inflow_outflow_precision: IMPOSSIBLE - delistings + privacy make tracking unreliable

cex_delisting_timeline.note: XMR removed from major exchanges over 2020-2023 period for regulatory reasons
cex_delisting_timeline.impact_on_research: Traditional CEX-based on-chain analysis (deposit/withdrawal flows) is unreliable; must rely on remaining DEX venues and transaction count as primary demand metrics

research_conclusion: Monero's privacy design is working: holder analysis is cryptographically impossible and expected. Transaction count trend (25k-41k daily, rising in 2025) and network hashrate are the only reliable on-chain indicators. Demand appears healthy based on sustained/rising transaction activity despite regulatory headwinds and CEX delistings.
caveat: All quantitative XMR analysis is limited by design to transaction count and hashrate. The absence of holder/whale/flow data is not a research gap—it is the intended privacy guarantee.
